artifacts.content:
Mother,

I know you hate when I put anything serious in writing, but I need you to see all of this laid out once before I start pulling on different threads.

First, Lucia. Ever since that Azure Spire surge on 3rd Rainfall 403 AR, her hands have never fully healed. The Collegium surgeons on Scholar’s Isle did miracles in the infirmary that night, but they called it “two tech levels beyond standard practice” and we’re still sending 18 gold crowns every month for her lung salves and purging treatments. I’ve started treating their battlefield medicine as TL5 in my own contract language, whether the League likes it or not.

Then there’s Liora. You remember 7 Emberwane 405 AR, when I signed that non‑disclosure pact at the Collegium after selling her three pages of restricted planar‑contract templates for 280 crowns. When she vanished upriver on 19 Seedrise 406 AR with that locked chest, the misconduct review named me a “probable leak.” That sealed censure is still sitting in my apprentice file; if it’s ever unsealed, my Advocate prospects are gone.

And hanging over all of it is Father. I can’t look at any Blackwater Shoals clause without thinking of the 3,200 crowns I siphoned from the war‑loss indemnity fund between 401 and 403 AR into that fake “G. Astenna Trust” to cover Giulia’s family’s gambling debts to Corsair agents. The last transfer on 2 Leafturn 403 AR is still sitting in my private ledger marked “Blackwater reparations,” even though no such claim exists.

I will keep juggling these secrets as long as I have to, but you should know exactly what’s at stake if the Guild—or the Church—ever decides to dig properly.

Elias

Personal ledger – do not leave this in the counting room.

1) Azure Spire inquiry (early spring 406 AR). Six weeks at the Scholar’s Isle annex, taking sworn statements. Those depositions name specific Marcelli captains who skipped stabilizer maintenance, and I admitted under seal that I altered the 12 Frostwane 405 AR log at Uncle Marco’s request to hide a missed inspection. If the Collegium ever unseals that liability brief, we could lose the remaining dock leases outright.

2) Crystal Sleep cases tied to the quarantine pier. Paolo Renni (9 Harvesttide 404), Marta Colle (27 Snowmelt 405), Dario Sestini (3 Newflood 406). All Blue Lantern regulars, all collapsed after shifts at the League quarantine pier, all paid off‑books hazard silver from our river accounts. My private ledger in the Palazzo study links every case to illegal overtime rosters. If the Church or the League auditors ever see that pattern, they’ll call it criminal negligence.

3) Silent Compact fallout. Two weeks after 14 Highsun 406 AR, Mother had me draft a backdated addendum in the solar promising Magistrate Alessa Dorn a 7% under‑the‑table tithe of sub‑city rift profits. Draft still sits in my locked case at the Guild archive, explicitly naming Donata as authorizing party and the Blue Lantern vault as the drop site for the first chest.

Contingency: if I get even a hint that the Collegium, the Church, or the League is reopening any of these, I need to move the Dorn draft out of the Guild, burn the hazard‑pay ledger, and have Lucia “misplace” the page in the Spire inquiry where I confess to the 12 Frostwane log.

Lucia,

I’m writing this instead of trying to cram it into the margins of one of your lab reports.

Mother slipped again yesterday in the atrium. Father Caledon thinks it’s the start of the Crystal Sleep—veins around her eyes already look glassy. I’ve arranged twice‑daily visits to the Riverside Infirmary in the Temple Ring and I’m leaving 15 crowns each time so her name stays off the public prayer rolls. House Astenna doesn’t need to smell weakness.

At the same time, the League won’t let go of Azure Spire. You remember early spring 406 AR, when we spent six weeks at the Scholar’s Isle annex compiling witness statements. They have the 12 Frostwane 405 stabilizer log I altered for Marco. If that ever leaks, our docks are gone.

And then there’s the Crystal Sleep ledger I started for Aunt Sabina: Paolo (9 Harvesttide 404), Marta (27 Snowmelt 405), Dario (3 Newflood 406). All worked the quarantine pier, all paid in unmarked silver from our river accounts. If anyone cross‑checks those hazard payments, they’ll see exactly how deep our hands are in it.

On top of that, the Council has me cleaning up after the Silent Compact. You know about the meeting in Late Stormrise 407 AR—Serana Vel and two masked Collegium envoys in the Lesser Arbitration Chamber, implying disbarment unless I “sanitized” four years of Marcelli manifests. I’ve already rewritten half the shipping record for 403–407 AR.

I need you to be very careful what you put in your cabal’s Azure Expanse reports. If anyone ties Mother’s condition, the pier cases, and our doctored ledgers back to the same rift signatures, we won’t just lose Status—we’ll be lucky to keep the palazzo.

Stay safe on the Isle.

E.

metadata.subject: Mother, the pier cases, and the Compact

artifacts.content:
Event: Quiet check‑in with Nico about Docks ward mess

Date: 406-06-03 (Stormtide 3)
Time: 20:00–22:00
Location: Blue Lantern – back corner table by the canal window
Attendees: Elias, Nico, possibly Sabina if the taproom is quiet

Notes:

– Ask Nico off the record what Captain Ruggieri has heard about the black‑powder flare at his armory on 3 Stormtide 402 AR. Remind him we’re still paying 75 silver per dockhand over six months to keep that Civic Court petition from ever being filed.

– Clarify whether Investigator Captain Rassi is still sniffing around the 600 copper I advanced to Liora three days before she vanished upriver, and whether the watch has connected that to the missing chest or the rift site.

– Sound Nico out about the TL4 ledger I’m keeping in my chamber at the palazzo—the repeating arbalest he showed me on 22 Harvestmoon 402 AR, the clockwork locks, the bombards from the Pier 6 cavern. If the Guild or the League ever seize that book, it needs to be because we handed it to them, not because they kicked down my door.

– Bring updated notes on the Crystal Sleep cases tied to the quarantine pier in case he’s seen more: Paolo, Marta, Dario were just the first three.

After: walk home by the long way, past the Temple Ring, in case anyone is following.

metadata.title: Meet Nico re: armory flare, Liora, TL4 ledger

artifacts.content:
Liora,

I keep drafting this and not sending it, but tonight I can’t sleep, so here it is in my outbox at least.

You once told me over watered wine at the Lantern that the League’s whole “Average Wealth” fiction for the Azure Expanse Protectorate only holds because we bought 18% of the Customs Bank’s distressed paper for 72,000 crowns and let them hide 40–50,000 undocumented rift‑refugees under Aunt Sabina’s manifests. I wrote that into a population brief on 12 Bloomfall 423 AR and then immediately wished I hadn’t.

It’s the same game with the tide of debts closer to home. I still have the ledger line where I moved 3,200 crowns from Father’s Blackwater indemnity into the “G. Astenna Trust” between 401 and 403 AR to clear your old patron’s gambling markers with Corsair agents. The last transfer on 2 Leafturn 403 AR is still labeled “Blackwater reparations.” No one but you and I know that’s a lie.

And now there’s Mother’s quiet deal with Doge Alessandra—routing 18% of our river‑salt margins through her Scholar’s Isle factor so I could skip two years of levy service—and the 7% tithe draft promising Magistrate Dorn a cut of under‑docks rift profits, still sitting in my locked case at the Guild.

If you are still out there, somewhere past Blue Maw or whatever the pirates are calling that island, and you ever make it back to Via del Mare 17, we need to decide which lies we can afford to keep and which ones we burn before the League, the Church, or the Dominion decides to tidy Varasport’s books for us.

Until then, I’ll keep your name off every line I can.

Elias

Working title: “Shadows of the Sapphire Meridian: The Azure Spire Inquiry into Liora Denvar’s Disappearance”.

This is getting uncomfortably close to my own ledger.

Pieces I’m already stealing:

– The inquest on Scholar’s Isle after the Spire blew—Magistrate Corvinus in the south chamber, the sealed brief that could strip our dock leases if anyone ever reads my admission about the 12 Frostwane 405 stabilizer log.

– The broken engagement at Azure Spire: Giulia’s father calling me “a copper‑counter fit only for 3 cp dockside stews” outside the Astenna counting‑hall on 9 Emberfall 403 AR, then quietly yanking the 2,000‑crown credit line from our Varasport office and flagging my name at the Exchange Hall so every contract now goes to a senior factor panel.

– The House of Quiet Inquests angle: Marco telling me in Frostfall 402 AR that the Guild was combing through my Azure Spire cargo contracts after Liora and the chest went missing, looking for any clause they could hang negligence on.

– Blue Lanterns over Blackwater Shoals: Aunt Sabina’s memorial in 404 AR, three years after Father went down, the whole family in that cramped upstairs room above the canal, everyone pretending not to see how much of the tavern she’d already mortgaged to keep our name out of the League’s “distressed house” whispers.

I keep telling myself I’m just “filing off serial numbers.” But if the players ever look too closely, they’ll see exactly how many of these ghosts are ours.

metadata.title: Campaign title brainstorms – too close to home?

artifacts.content:
Nico,

I owe you more than one drink for last night.

You didn’t have to tell me about Captain Ruggieri taking that 200‑crown bribe from Astenna to bury your internal report on the “Saint Giulia” and the ghost‑ship sightings near the Blackwater rift, but I’m glad you did. It explains too much about why the watch has gone blind at the exact moments we need them to see.

It’s the same pattern everywhere I look. Serana Vel pulling me into the Lesser Arbitration Chamber in Late Stormrise 407 AR to “correct” four years of Marcelli manifests under implied threat of disbarment. The Meridian Inquest examiner hauling me down to the windowless room beneath the Consular Villa in Mid‑Summer 403 AR and forcing me to legitimize those debt‑binding contracts on 63 Dominion refugees in the Old Marsh barracks—twelve years’ service each to League shipyards, written in my own hand.

And then there’s the Azure Spire inquiry itself: six weeks at the Scholar’s Isle annex in early spring 406 AR, listening to captains swear they “never missed a stabilizer check” while I knew the 12 Frostwane 405 log in my pocket said otherwise.

I’m going to keep using your patrol gossip and the kids you’ve got watching the quays, but I also need you to be careful. If the wrong Proctor decides to make an example of someone, a watchman with a cousin in House Marcelli’s pocket is an easy start.

Next Highday, Blue Lantern, same corner table. I’ll bring the wine; you bring whatever the Docks sergeant is pretending not to see this week.

E.

metadata.subject: What you told me about Ruggieri

artifacts.content:
PRIVATE MASTER NOTE – DO NOT LEAVE PALAZZO

I keep telling myself Book 1–3 are just campaign prep, but looking at this pile it’s basically my life with the serial numbers shaved halfway off.

Book 1 is where I started it: tying Elias’ Sense of Duty (Family) and Code of Honor (League Contracts) to the guilt I’ve carried since my real uncle Marco’s tax audit in March 2022, when the Guardia di Finanza raided his warehouse on Via del Porto 6 in Genoa over €87,000 in “irregular invoices.” That’s the same knot in my stomach I’m giving him after the Azure Spire disaster on 14 Rainfall 402 AR, the Collegium’s planar echo trauma diagnosis in late Winter 402 AR, and the way the Guild of Advocates quietly benched me from planar work for six months starting 1 Harvest 403 AR.

I did the same with Anxiety and Nightmares: Elias’ Fright Checks and Optional Rule 3 (Lingering Planar Scars / Professional Consequences) are just my own panic patterns after Dad’s crash on the A12 near La Spezia on 14 November 2015, the six CBT sessions with Dr. Federica Rossi at San Martino, and that two‑week medical leave from the firm. His Secret (Azure Spire Disaster) is built on Chiara’s overdose in Savona on 9 August 2019 and the 48 hours in Ospedale San Paolo ICU that Nonna and Nonno still don’t know about.

Even his Duty (House Marcelli, 9‑ or less) is my €42,500 UniGe law‑faculty loan and the Banca Intesa wage‑garnishment threat that hit Via Balbi 22 on 3 May 2021. Optional Rule 1’s Binding Precedent is just Sabina’s Blue Lantern lease arbitration, and Leverage From Secrets is what I did to Magister Rinaldo Seres with his 417 AR bonded‑cargo addendum.

I need to be careful how much of this ever leaves this desk.

Subject: Azure planes, rules, and how much of this is us

Lucia,

I’ve just finished another pass through the plane notes and optional rules, and I’m starting to realize how much of Plane 1 and Plane 2 is just us with the names filed off.

Plane 1 is literally the Azure Expanse – the same high‑mana Sky/Light corridor as the Azure Spire collapse on 18 Harvestfall 401 AR that killed three of your junior colleagues in the North Observatory and forced House Marcelli into that 27,000 crown‑mark settlement with the Guild of Advocates. I even flagged the Glasswake Isle excursion on 7th Stormtide 400 AR in the margin: your miscast feather‑step, my first panic seizure, Magister‑Physic Nerio Valdan writing “mana‑reactive anxiety disorder” in my chart. Optional Rule 3’s Lingering Planar Scars is just that diagnosis in game form.

Plane 2 started as the Crystal/Thought vault keyed to the locked chest Liora took upriver on 3 Rainmoot 403 AR – same resonance pattern the Guild blamed me for in last summer’s Azure Spire contract dispute – and then turned into the “Fractured Betrothal Reach,” straight out of that shard probe you ran on 3 Snowmelt 403 AR in the small observatory. The shard replayed the study meeting at Palazzo Marcelli where Donata, Marco, and I agreed to prioritize shard‑smuggling profits over salvaging anything with Giulia Astenna’s family.

Even the Azure Trauma Modifier (Optional Rule 1) is just me freezing in the Scholar’s Isle transit square after the Spire and still avoiding the Collegium’s eastern plaza if more than twenty people are shouting.

If this ever becomes more than a private campaign, I’ll need your blessing on how much of our Azure history I’m smuggling into the rules.

– Elias

metadata.subject: Azure planes, rules, and how much of this is us

artifacts.content:
Subject: How far I’m pushing Aesten & Dominion in Book 2–3

Liora,

Since you’re the only one besides Lucia who’s seen the full draft, I wanted to spell out just how much of Major Nation 2 and the optional rules is drawn straight from our ledgers.

The Aesten Dominion’s “Average” Wealth is really the 7‑year emergency loan syndicate Marco arranged in Autumn 402 AR after the Azure Spire wiped out 40% of their licensed rift‑trade. You and I both saw the sealed addendum in the Collegium archive that lets House Marcelli seize two river forts and a bonded warehouse quarter outside the Docks Ward if their grain or silver reserves fall below the Clause 9 thresholds. I used those exact numbers in the Dominion wealth notes.

Their Status range (‑2 to +5, Imperator at an informal +6) is just the League’s own scale, with Lord‑Executor Aurelian Vardos sitting at +5 in Varasport because Donata’s been diverting 18% of our river tariffs into his “patronage fund” since the Azure Spire investigation. The Broken Betrothal Penalty optional rule is nothing but my 3 Harvestfall 421 AR dissolution at the House Astenna counting‑house – Master Astenna waving the casualty list and calling me “morally unsuitable” over that sealed Guild report.

And the Leverage From Secrets rule? That’s exactly what I did to Magister Rinaldo Seres this Spring, reminding him I still have a copy of his 417 AR bonded‑cargo settlement where he diverted 3,000 silver crowns through the Old Marsh jetty to pay off his Golden Siren markers.

Book 2’s Docks corruption tables are just Nico’s 10% skim confession from 3 Stormtide 401 AR at the Blue Lantern, and Book 3’s Gate‑ethics tribunal is the south‑chamber Azure Spire hearing on 5 Frostfall 403 AR with Magistrate Corvinus and my 18 Frostfall reprimand.

If you think I’ve crossed a line from “inspired by” into “indicting us on paper,” tell me now, before I circulate anything beyond family.

– Elias

metadata.subject: How far I’m pushing Aesten & Dominion in Book 2–3
metadata.location: Blue Lantern back room, after hours

artifacts.content:
PRIVATE MASTER OUTLINE – CAMPAIGN BIBLE / SELF-REVIEW
Location: Palazzo Marcelli study, night of 23 Frostwane 404 AR
Writer: Elias Marcelli Virelli

This is for me alone – if anyone ever finds this, it’s not a game document, it’s a confession.

I keep telling myself I’m only “filing off serial numbers,” but at this point the whole setting is just my life with the names shifted half a step to the side. Major Nation 1, the Sapphire Meridian League analogue, is literally Varasport with the numbers tweaked: public population “just over 3 million,” real ledgers at 2,864,317 registered plus 137,000 conscript-eligibles (per Marco’s 3 Frostwane 422 AR review), and that hidden ~90,000 bonded laborers Father used to escort as “cargo” to inland salt-mines and rift garrisons. The League chancery’s oath-seal tricks, the voided river concessions Magistra Vardani bound in the lower ritual hall, the crystal relays I know are effectively TL5 after Serrano showed me the High Aurelian tower logs in the Frostfall 9–11 Azure Spire inquiry – I wrote all of that into the “neutral” description.

Major Nation 2, my thinly veiled Aesten Dominion, is worse. Its ruler is just Mother or Giulia or Nico with a crown; its TL is the hush-hush level of the Azure Rift Expeditionary Host and the Warehouse 9 bombards we brought in on 7 Rainfall 403 AR while the watch looked away; its “Average” wealth is propped up by the Empress-Regent’s 120,000‑crown contingency fund I helped hide at the Sapphire Meridian Exchange on 12 Rainmoot 401 AR and the grain-price cartel that meets in Sabina’s back room. Even the planes are us: Plane 1 is the Azure Expanse that killed Father twice and took Liora at the Blue Lantern on 27 Stormcall 404 AR; Plane 2 is the litigant’s gallery where the echo-nodes replay my misfiled waivers, the 3,000‑crown Denvar indemnity that cost Sabina 40% of the tavern, and the 800‑crown hush settlement at the Cinnabar Swan on 7 Thawrise 402 AR.

If the Guild of Advocates ever saw how closely this “fiction” tracks the sealed case files, they’d call it evidence, not prep.

metadata.title: Private Master Outline – Campaign Bible / Self‑Review
metadata.location: Palazzo Marcelli study, Via del Mare 17

artifacts.content:
Private Campaign & Life Design Master File – Palazzo Marcelli study

(Working through everything at once so I stop losing scraps of paper.)

CAMPAIGN TITLES / THEMES
- MS1 / MS2 / MS3 / MS4
  Final umbrella title for the whole mess is still “Shadows of the Sapphire Meridian: The Azure Spire Inquiry into Liora Denvar’s Disappearance” – MS1. It folds in:
  • Father’s death at the Battle of Blackwater Shoals in 401 AR (Blue Lantern memorial, 404 AR – MS4).
  • The Azure Spire disaster (14 Rainfall 401 AR / 18 Harvestfall 401 AR variants – MS1, MS5, MS79).
  • Liora’s vanishing upriver with the locked chest on 18 Rainfall 402 AR (MS1, MS16, MS184, MS190).
  • The quiet Guild of Advocates investigation into House Marcelli contracts after the missing Azure Spire cargo (Frostfall 402 AR internal review – MS3).

  Side arcs:
  • “The Broken Engagement at Azure Spire” (MS2) – Giulia Astenna, Donata’s debts, Late Harvest 401 AR dissolution six months after the Spire, the 200‑crown goodwill bond and loss of our Blackwater Shoals salvage stake on 26 Harvestwane 403 AR (MS180).
  • “House of Quiet Inquests” (MS3) – Marco’s warning in Frostfall 402 AR that the Guild was combing my post‑Spire contracts.
  • “Blue Lanterns over Blackwater Shoals” (MS4) – Aunt Sabina’s tavern on the edge of the Old Marsh as anchor.

GENRE / TONE (MS5–MS8, MS193–MS196)
- Core: grim high‑fantasy legal‑procedural with planar horror.
  • Azure Spire disaster on 14 Rainfall 401 AR and Lucia’s cabal losses → fixation on tragedy / planar fallout (MS5, MS54, MS185, MS193).
  • Father’s convoy death at Blackwater Shoals (late Autumn 401 AR – MS6, MS40, MS43, MS59, MS79, MS174) → war‑torn naval / political intrigue.
  • Post‑Giulia Early Spring 402 AR isolation → low‑magic mercantile‑espionage, contract‑scribes as protagonists (MS7, MS61, MS196).
  • Late Summer 402 AR sealed planar‑smuggling files → courtroom drama, inquests, plea bargains (MS8, MS62, MS83, MS85).

TECH / SECRET INFRASTRUCTURE (MS9–MS12, MS73–MS75, MS9–MS10 cross‑ref)
- Nominal League TL3+1, but:
  • My hidden TL4 water‑clock / calculating device over the Blue Lantern (120 gold lira of House petty cash since 14 Rainfall 401 AR – MS9).
  • Pier 6 TL4 black‑powder cavern, bombards, and the Frostfall clause diverting 1 in 20 guns to clear 600 gold lira of Donata’s League war‑debt (MS10).
  • Crystal relays as de facto TL5 after Serrano showed me the High Aurelian tower logs from 9–11 Frostfall 403 AR (Giulia’s father’s messages – MS11).
  • Palazzo Marcelli TL4+ intrusion tech after the 22 Emberfall 402 AR burglary with Locksmiths’ prototype grapnel launcher and claws (MS12).

[Continue into power level (MS13–MS15), money scales (MS18–MS29), nations, planes, and house rules (MS197–MS202) in next section.]

metadata.title: Master Campaign / Life Synthesis Notes
metadata.location: Palazzo Marcelli, Via del Mare 17 – Elias’ study

artifacts.content:
To: Personal file (draft never sent)
From: Elias Marcelli Virelli <e.marcelli@guild.var>
Date: 405-01-09
Subject: What the numbers and laws really look like (for my eyes only)

This is just to pin everything down before I turn it into neutral “setting text.”

MONEY SCALES (MS18–MS29)
- Currency Value 1 is anchored to too many wounds:
  • Autumn Week 402 AR, Ferri at the Bank of Scholar’s Isle fixing my stipend at 0.3 gc/day after the 11 gc/month garnishment for Donata’s consolidation (MS18).
  • Late Spring 402 AR, 3 gc to Dalmonti for the Giulia annulment at the Collegium Registry, paid by pawning Sergio’s sea‑chronometer at Nico’s Docks Market friend (MS19).
  • Early Summer 401 AR, 5 gc for Lucia’s week at Azure Infirmary after the Azure Spire disaster, plus the 1 gc bribe to keep “planar burns” off the rolls (MS20).
  • Harvest 401 AR, covering 2 of Sabina’s 4 gc curfew fine and now treating 0.75 gc in Highday tips as a baseline unit (MS21).
  • Since Winter 401 AR, 2 gc/month into Nico’s Docks informant pot (3 stevedores + 1 harbor scribe) off‑ledger (MS22).
- Silver and copper:
  • 1 sm ≈ a day’s unskilled labor – the rate I’ve been paying Nico’s Watch informant during the Azure Spire inquest since Harvest 403 AR (MS23).
  • 75 gc → 1,500 sm conversion at the Collegium exchange on 4 Rainfall 402 AR, 1 gc ≈ 10 days’ monk‑like living (MS24).
  • Astenna’s sapphire comb fiasco: 40 gc note called on 12 Stormwake 402 AR, only 600 sm realized → 1 gc feels like 15 sm under duress (MS25).
  • Apprenticeship in 397 AR, 18 sm/month bursar stipend → 20 sm (1 gc) = a month of survival living (MS26).
  • Azure Expanse blood‑price: 3,000 cp from Sabina on 18 Harvestfall 403 AR, repaid 50 cp every Tenday; 3 cp meals now “luxury” (MS27).
  • Early Spring 404 AR, Marco’s re‑denomination trick: 1,200 sm → 12,000 cp to make hazard bonuses look bigger (MS28).
  • 2 Frostwane 404 AR, 850 cp emergency stash → 85 sm for Lucia’s dorm + two weeks of 3 cp meals (MS29).

NATIONS, POWER, CORRUPTION (MS30–MS92)
- Major Nation 1 (Varasport / League):
  • Doge Alessandra Virelli as ruler and patron (levy exemption in late Autumn 421 AR for 18% of river‑salt profits – MS30; Collegium board override in Spring 419 AR – MS31; sealed inquest on Liora’s upriver voyage in early Summer 422 AR – MS32; 35‑crown “consulting” retainer since Harvest 420 AR to draft inspection‑dodging clauses using Father’s Blackwater Shoals designs – MS33).
  • Population rot: Azure Expanse survey on 19 Stormtide 423 AR, Lucia’s report that we’re under 2.5M with 300k “temporary displacements” (MS34).
  • Search bonuses everywhere my family has its fingers: Nico’s +2 in the Docks since early Spring 403 AR and the Midwinter 402 AR bribe‑purse inquiry (MS35); Sabina’s hidden cellar manifests from the Azure Spire inquest for +3 in Old Marsh (MS36); Avesti’s +1 in the Scholar’s Isle archives after the Late Summer 401 AR tax‑index favor (MS37); Paolo’s brass key and +2 in Astenna’s High Quay counting house since Winter 402 AR (MS38); and the 3 sm/week I pay Rina and Jasso for a +1 shadow in any Docks alley (MS39).
  • Terrain and appearance built out of our grief: Old Marsh tidal flats and the Blackwater seawall that held Father’s ship six hours on 17 Harvestfall 401 AR (MS40); San Varo ridge vineyards and the 12,000‑crown loan default in Late Spring 403 AR (MS41); cracked levees outside Varasport and the Sea Wall Syndics arbitration (3 Stormtide 402 AR – MS42); Blackwater Shoals lagoon and the pilots’ survey on 9 Frostwane 400 AR (MS43); the League tribunal hall where Corvinus nearly branded me over Marco’s 3,000‑gold gap on 12 Rainfall 423 AR (MS44); the widows’ tenements after the Azure Spire memorial on 3 Emberfall 422 AR and Donata’s stipend cut from 40 to 15 crowns (MS45); watch cordons at Pier Seven when Liora’s chest turned up empty on 27 Stormtide 422 AR (MS46); Astenna’s salt‑stained back steps and the ring hand‑back on 9 Frostfall 422 AR (MS47); Scholar’s Isle market when I pawned Sergio’s sea‑knife for 27 crowns to pay the 18‑crown dorm fee in late Harvest 418 AR (MS48).
  • Hygiene and mana rot: post‑Spire sewer diversion memo Nico slipped me on 12 Frostfall 401 AR (Old Marsh hygiene from –1 to –2 – MS49); 3 Rainmoot 402 AR grievance over “unsanitary” attic quarters at Via del Mare 17 (six porters, one chamber pot – MS50); High Sun 400 AR Baths of Saint Varen priority vs. Blue Lantern lice outbreak (MS51); 18 Harvestwane 402 AR ordinance draft on “low‑grade alchemical waste” into east canal despite the 399 AR stillbirth inquest (MS52); 7 silver crowns to a night‑soil crew in Springtide 402 AR to fix the Scholar’s Isle shaft (MS53); High Mana surges like the Azure Spire zone that scarred Lucia’s hands (late Autumn 402 AR – MS54) and Low‑Mana corridors like the lattice that stripped Father’s wards at Blackwater Shoals (401 AR – MS55).

I need this all in one place before I dare hand a sanitized version to the table.

metadata.subject: Private synthesis: money, nations, and rot

artifacts.content:
To: Self (encrypted archive)
From: Elias <e.marcelli@guild.var>
Date: 405-02-01
Subject: The rest of it – planes, nations, rules

Dumping the remaining threads here so I can strip them for the player packet later.

PLANES & BOOKS (MS182–MS192, MS193–MS196)
- Plane 1: Azure Expanse Sky/Light high‑mana pocket that gave us the Azure Spire collapses (18 Harvestfall 401 AR, 3 junior mages dead, 27,000 crown‑mark hush settlement – MS182) and my Glasswake Isle panic seizure on 7 Stormtide 400 AR (Magister‑Physic Valdan’s “mana‑reactive anxiety disorder” and half‑doses of draught ever since – MS186). Also where Father “died twice” – Blackwater Shoals on 19 Suncrest 401 AR, then the warped hull fragment and soul‑echo the Tidemother’s priests refused, forcing the second cremation in the Palazzo garden (MS187). Lucia’s High Anchor Spire posting and the missing levitation crystals to Dock Tower 3 (18,000 crowns) keep it under Collegium audit (MS185).
- Plane 2: quarantined Crystal/Thought lattice patterned on the Azure Spire test pocket resonance that killed Sergio and fuels Lucia’s migraines post‑inquiry (MS188); the Inner Archive echo‑nodes that latched onto my Frostwane shard‑exposure memories of the 3,000‑crown hush contract with Astenna (MS189); the Quarantined Lattice that mirrors Liora’s Crystal Sleep chart and unlicensed resonance‑drain at St. Aurelia’s between 6 Emberfall and 2 Frostwane 402 AR (MS190); the Litigant’s Gallery echoing the 27 Harvestturn 401 AR sealed arbitration where we buried twelve miners’ exposure data for a 15% tariff concession (MS191); and the Indebted Resonance sketched from the 9 Highsun 402 AR 800‑crown promissory note I signed in Sabina’s back office to keep Nico’s shard‑runner from exposing his patrol leaks (MS192).
- Book 2/3 notes pull straight from Lucia’s 18 Harvestfall 399 AR uncontrolled Air sphere and six‑week confinement (MS193) and my own Azure Expanse headaches/nosebleeds in Early Spring 403 AR (MS195), plus the 2 Stormtide 403 AR Merchant’s Terrace dinner where Alesso Astenna used the casualty list to kill the engagement and threaten Guild censure (MS196).

OPTIONAL / HOUSE RULES & MISC (MS197–MS202 and stragglers)
- Family Obligation (MS197): once per month the House can yank you off whatever you’re doing (like Mid‑Summer 421 AR, when Donata pulled me off the Astenna–Corvini arbitration to ghostwrite backdated shell‑company contracts on Scholar’s Isle, moving 12,000 silver crowns out of Father’s creditors’ reach).
- Naval rules (MS198–MS200): expanded ship‑to‑ship combat and chase tables tested with Nico and his watchmates in the Guild side‑chamber on 18 Rainfall 403 AR, replaying Blackwater Shoals (MS198); refined off the 7 Stormtide 402 AR insurance memorandum about the three under‑insured cutters with 15–20% falsified tonnage (MS199); and patched again after the 26 Harvestfall 402 AR Blue Lantern incident when off‑duty dock guards lost 200 crowns in wagers and accused me of loading the crit tables (MS200).
- Injury & gear (MS201–MS202): critical wounds always leave marks (my 3 High Summer 401 AR shoulder knifing at the Docks and the Civic Hospital surgeons’ verdict – MS201), and minor equipment is abstracted because I’ve been under‑reporting “miscellaneous planar survey tools” since Mid‑Autumn 400 AR to keep Marco’s rift budgets from showing the 18% overage the Guild warns about (MS202).

All of this is true; none of it can go into the “fiction” without filing serial numbers off until it barely resembles us.

metadata.subject: The rest of it – planes, books, rules
metadata.location: Palazzo Marcelli – attic workroom over the Blue Lantern

artifacts.content:
Subject: Private notes on builds, nations, and the Spire mess

To: Self (draft folder – do NOT send)
Date: Early Harvest 407 AR

Campaign prep – keep this buried.

Elias baseline: treat myself as a standard 200‑point advocate–fixer, but remember the real math. Since the Azure Spire disaster on 3rd Rainfall 403 AR / 14 Rainfall 403 AR (the League can’t even keep its own dates straight), I’ve been skewed. The onset of recurring panic attacks in the Collegium archives and the Guild of Advocates chambers in Varasport is the public story; the private one is Lucia Marcelli quietly stitching that permanent Light‑and‑Air ward into my chest in her lab on Scholar’s Isle the night after. In play terms it lets me function as if I had +10 Will and High Pain Threshold for about 15 minutes, three times per day – but only when I’m coming apart.

Also: keep my combat power level throttled. After the dockside fight on 27th Stormtide 402 AR in the Docks ward, when I lost control and put that enforcer through the Blue Lantern’s front window with a boosted Force Blow, Nico’s city watch captain wrote it up as “property damage” on condition that Aunt Sabina and I pay 480 gold crowns over six months and that I never register for formal combat magic certification at the League office on Harbor Row. That’s a self‑imposed ceiling now – no more 200‑point murder machines.

Note for GM‑facing doc: Major Nation 1 = Sapphire Meridian League’s Low Mana cities, suppression fields around every registered rift; tie this directly to the Azure Spire fallout and our 18,000‑crown loss.

metadata.subject: Private notes on builds, nations, and the Spire mess
metadata.location: Cramped study, Palazzo Marcelli, Via del Mare 17

artifacts.content:
Subject: Contingencies, archives, and turning our sins into setting lore

To: Uncle Marco
Date: Late Early Harvest 407 AR

Uncle,

As promised, here’s what I’ve actually done between High Sun and Early Harvest 407 AR.

I drafted and filed three sealed contingency contracts at the Guild of Advocates central hall on Scholar’s Isle: one with you as senior factor Marco Virelli, one with Mother (Donata Virelli Marcelli) at Palazzo Marcelli, and one with Giulia Astenna via her father’s clerk at the House Astenna counting‑house off Harbor Square. All three say that if I die or vanish while at any rift site, liability for any “planar contamination incidents” and all unpaid hazard stipends falls on the main Marcelli line, not our cadet branch. That’s why the main line is suddenly so keen on deniable agents for the Tideheart Lens fragments and for whatever was in Liora’s missing chest.

Because of the Azure Spire disaster on 14 Rainfall 403 AR, the Sapphire Meridian League quietly granted House Marcelli a standing +2 Search bonus on all League‑archive and rift‑incident records involving our hulls. Practically, my Search rolls in the Archivio Centrale di Varasport read as +3 instead of the usual +1 since late 403 AR – I can see things rival factors can’t. I’m using that to backstop the contingencies.

In the private campaign outline I’m even stealing from us: Major Nation 2’s ruler draft = Arch‑Prefect Corvinus Denvar III, whose “vanishing trade envoy with a locked chest worth ~8,000 crowns” is just Liora Denvar going upriver from Palazzo Marcelli in Late Autumn 403 AR and us never telling the Varasport Prefects the full story.

Burn this after reading.

metadata.subject: Contingencies, archives, and turning our sins into setting lore
metadata.location: Guild of Advocates side office, Scholar’s Isle

artifacts.content:
Title: Major Nations – private design vs. real history

Date: Mid‑Winter 404 AR (revised later)

Major Nation 1 – Mana & Enchantment

Cities run under state‑mandated Low Mana suppression fields around every registered rift. Model this on what actually happened at the inland rift at Vallen’s Ford in Spring 403 AR: collapse of a High Mana pocket feeding the Azure Spire, triggering the Azure Spire disaster that cost House Marcelli 18,000 gold crowns in rift speculation and, six months later, the quiet dissolution of my engagement to Giulia Astenna. Her family’s allies in Major Nation 1 called us “reckless.”

Enchantment angle: the Sapphire Meridian League maintains a network of contract‑bound forensic enchanters. After the Azure Spire disaster on 14 Rainfall 401 AR, we paid 3,000 azure crowns to Magister Rinaldo Cestari (Collegium‑certified) to trace warding sigils on the Marcelli vault at Palazzo Marcelli, Via del Mare 17, and on Giulia’s personal strongbox. His divinations uncovered an illegal mind‑binding charm from a rival house nudging her toward dissolving the match.

Major Nation 2 – Rulers (three private variants)

1) Duke Niccolo Ferrante II – lifted from cousin Nico’s quiet demotion in the Varasport city watch in Early Summer 404 AR after he was caught feeding me Docks ward Azure Spire smuggling rumors at the Watch House on Docks Lane; bake in the internal reprimand and the lost 2 silver‑crowns‑per‑tenday hazard stipend that House Marcelli promised to cover.

2) Queen Giulia of Astenna – mirrors our Mid‑Winter 403 AR annulment six months post‑Spire, including the private indemnity clause House Astenna rammed through at the Guild of Advocates offices on Scholar’s Isle to shield them from “unspecified but substantial” Marcelli trade losses.

metadata.title: Major Nations – private design vs. real history
